feature_name: v1.21.0 "The Polish Release": subscription count-edit bug fix; audio player overhaul with custom theme-aware blocky controls (click-art-to-play, persisted volume); subscriptions page rearchitect (subscriptions-first list + settings-detail surface); subscribed-on date + clickable channel link; pin-a-channel-playlist to persistent nav; library grid mobile density; extra-deliberate delete for local files; download retry + status chip; watch-page theatre mode; README refresh
base: main (v1.20.0). Prior shipped records live in docs/exec-plans/completed/ (most recently v1.20-subscribe).
process_plan: Discovery completed by product-manager, grounded directly in docs/ui-research-2026-07.md (mandated UI/UX research synthesis), ROADMAP.md's Planned section (every FR already listed there -- confirms no scope creep), the completed v1.16-watch-experience and v1.20-subscribe exec plans for precedent, and the live code (public/js/player.js in full, watch.html/subscriptions.html shell markup, lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js in full, lib/ytdlp/store.js + index.js's PATCH route, lib/ytdlp/activity.js in full, server.js's POST /api/config folder pruning + DELETE /api/videos/:id, watch.js/main.js delete flows, style.css's grid/thumbnail/audio-mode sections) -- not assumed from prior wording. Ten FRs, each grounded with a specific code-reading finding (see the exec plan's Decision log): FR-1's root cause (the subscriptions list's ~2.5s live-status poll unconditionally rebuilds every row, destroying an open/unsaved edit panel -- a client render-timing bug, not a server validation bug); FR-5's design constraint (POST /api/config prunes folderSettings to real db.folders entries + the one synthetic root, so a per-channel pin CANNOT reuse folderSettings and needs a new store); FR-6's narrowed remaining gap (aspect-ratio/object-fit already shipped; a v1.15.0 mobile grid rule already exists but Dean's on-device report says it's still not comfortable -- scope narrowed to further tightening); FR-2's iOS volume platform constraint (script cannot set HTMLMediaElement.volume on iOS Safari -- flagged so an on-device report doesn't mistake this for a regression); FR-7's reusable detection signal (db.metadata[id]'s channelUrl/channelId/channelName from v1.20 FR-2); FR-4's addedAt field already exists (no new stored field needed); FR-8's one-shot-jobs-have-no-retry-today gap (subscriptions already have Re-pull as an existing retry mechanism; one-shot jobs only have Dismiss). Two resolved (non-blocking) forks: FR-2's shared audio+video control-bar scope (one <video> element serves both -- build audio-first, verify video isn't regressed) and FR-5's pin-store keying (channelDir+label snapshot, not a live subscription-id join, so unsubscribing doesn't silently break a pin). No genuinely blocking fork surfaced. Review tiers: HEAVIEST two-reviewer/adversarial gate for FR-2 (shared-player regression risk spans both media types + the whole v1.16 persistent-shell/dock machinery); HEAVY two-reviewer gate for FR-3 (subscription CRUD UI, same tier as v1.20 FR-1/FR-4), FR-5 (folders/config invariant), and FR-7 (destructive delete); LIGHT single-QA gate for FR-1, FR-4, FR-6, FR-9, FR-10; LIGHT-TO-MEDIUM for FR-8. Dean's on-device pass is the documented arbiter (no headless-browser/E2E infra exists per docs/RELIABILITY.md) for FR-2 (audio, especially iOS Safari), FR-6 (grid density feel), FR-9 (theatre feel), and FR-3 (list/detail-page feel) -- not a testing gap.
hard_constraints: Additive / no regressions: every FR layers on existing behavior; FR-2 must preserve every existing player feature (inline iOS playback, +-15s skip, transcode overlay, resume overlay, the full v1.16 FULL/DOCKED/CLOSED state machine, Media Session, progress saving) across BOTH audio and video -- the two media types share one <video id="media-player"> element, so there is no way to touch one without touching the other.

Disabled-yt-dlp-module byte-identical guarantee preserved for FR-1/FR-3/FR-4/FR-5/FR-8: with FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED off, no new route/UI/DOM is reachable; /subscriptions still 404s server-side exactly as today.

db.folders/folderSettings synthetic-root invariant unchanged (FR-5): a pin is NEVER written into db.folders or folderSettings (POST /api/config prunes folderSettings to real db.folders entries + the one synthetic Downloads root -- a per-channel pin would be silently dropped if stored there); the pin store is never scanned/pruned as a folder.

No existing security guard weakened: FR-1/FR-3/FR-4/FR-5/FR-8 all route through the SAME, unmodified validateChannelUrl / validateSubscriptionInput / validateSubscriptionPatch / DELETE /api/videos/:id paths -- no new parallel validator, no bypass.

FR-7 must fail safe: the yt-dlp-vs-local detection signal (reusing db.metadata[id]'s channelUrl/channelId/channelName from v1.20 FR-2) must never misclassify toward LESS delete friction than today; any ambiguous/missing signal (including every pre-v1.20 local file) is always treated as local/irreplaceable.

Follow docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: vanilla DOM (no framework/bundler -- notably FR-2's custom control bar and FR-8's status chip), CommonJS server, 2-space/semicolons/single-quotes, textContent over innerHTML for new dynamic strings (FR-4's channel link/date, FR-3's detail surface, FR-5's pin labels, FR-8's chip text), node:test for new/regression tests, lint 0 warnings, NO new runtime dependencies.

Preserve the era-theme system (CSS custom properties) for all new CSS: FR-2's control bar, FR-3's list/detail surface, FR-6's grid tightening, FR-8's chip, FR-9's theatre layout -- no hardcoded colors breaking the 2005/2009/2014/2021 eras or light/dark modes.

Node.js 22 LTS (engines >=20); no headless-browser/E2E infrastructure exists (docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- Dean's on-device pass is the documented arbiter for FR-2 (audio, especially iOS Safari's fixed-volume platform constraint), FR-6 (grid density), FR-9 (theatre), and FR-3 (list/detail feel), not a testing gap to close.

FR-2's iOS constraint: iOS Safari does not permit script control of HTMLMediaElement.volume (fixed at 1.0, hardware buttons only) -- the custom volume control must be hidden or clearly inert-but-non-broken on iOS, never presented as a bug to fix.

product_forks_for_dean.fr2_audio_vs_video_control_scope: RESOLVED (non-blocking) -- one shared <video id="media-player"> element serves both audio and video (confirmed by reading player.js/watch.html/subscriptions.html), so removing native controls necessarily replaces video's control bar too. Recommended default: one shared, theme-aware/blocky control-bar component, built audio-first (Dean's asks are audio-centric) but explicitly verified not to regress video (fullscreen, rotate-to-fullscreen, skip-controls overlay, live-transcode). Not a genuine fork requiring a decision -- there is no way to fork the shared element without a much larger, out-of-scope refactor.
product_forks_for_dean.fr5_pin_store_keying: RESOLVED (non-blocking), PE may revise -- recommend storing a pin as a channelDir + display-label SNAPSHOT rather than a live join keyed solely on subscription id, so an unpinned-but-still-downloaded channel (unsubscribe without deleting files) doesn't silently break an existing pin. Subscribe (follow) and Pin (quick-access) are different intents per the UI research; exact persisted shape is PE's Design-stage call.
product_forks_for_dean.fr1_fr3_sequencing: RESOLVED, not a genuine fork -- FR-1 (the poll-clobbers-open-edit bug) must be fixed and independently verified regardless of FR-3's scope/timing per Dean's explicit instruction, even though FR-3's settings-detail-page redesign is expected to structurally obsolete the exact race condition as a side effect (a dedicated page/sheet is never subject to the subscriptions list's live poll re-render). Recommended sequencing: fix FR-1 first (small, fast, independently valuable), then FR-3 rebases on top of the fixed row-rendering code rather than a divergent version.

FR-1.title: [BUG] Subscription count-edit reverts
FR-1.review_weight: LIGHT single-QA gate -- server-side validator/mutator path (store.validateSubscriptionPatch/updateSubscription) is unchanged and already correct; this is purely a client-side render-timing fix (the ~2.5s live-status poll unconditionally rebuilds every row, destroying an open/unsaved edit panel).
files: test/unit + test/integration (regression: open-edit survives a live poll tick, for every edit-panel field, not just maxVideos)

FR-2.title: Audio player overhaul (custom blocky theme-aware controls; click-art-to-play; persisted volume)
FR-2.review_weight: HEAVIEST two-reviewer/adversarial gate in this round -- shared-player regression risk spans BOTH audio and video and the entire v1.16 FULL/DOCKED/CLOSED persistent-shell/dock state machine. Dean's on-device pass (especially iOS Safari) is the arbiter.
files: public/js/player.js (the v1.16 persistent player controller -- primary surface)
public/watch.html, public/index.html, public/setup.html, lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html (#player-host-template markup, replicated across all four shells)
test/unit (volume clamp/parse helper, seek-scrub-vs-commit reducer, any other extracted pure helper) + test/integration where applicable

FR-3.title: Subscriptions page rearchitect (subscriptions-FIRST list + settings-detail surface)
FR-3.review_weight: HEAVY two-reviewer gate (subscription CRUD UI surface -- same tier as v1.20's FR-1/FR-4). Depends on FR-1 landing first (same primary file).
files: lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js (major rewrite -- row rendering, new settings-detail surface)
test/unit + test/integration (row rendering, tap-to-open-channel, kebab-to-settings, disabled-module no-op)

FR-4.title: "Subscribed to" date + clickable channel link
FR-4.review_weight: LIGHT single-QA gate -- display-only, reuses the already-persisted addedAt field and the already-validateChannelUrl-validated channelUrl (no new trust boundary). Rides FR-3's row-rendering work (same file, bundle together).
files: test/unit (date formatting incl. missing-addedAt fallback, href-safe link construction)

FR-5.title: Pin a channel's "View as Playlist" to persistent nav
FR-5.review_weight: HEAVY two-reviewer gate (data-safety / folders-config invariant) -- must prove the new pin store never touches db.folders/folderSettings and is never pruned by POST /api/config.
files: lib/ytdlp/store.js and/or a new lib/ytdlp module (new pin store -- NOT folderSettings)
public/js/common.js (Playlists sheet rendering of pinned entries)
lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js (pin toggle on a subscription row -- after FR-3 lands)
test/unit + test/integration (pin persistence, db.folders/folderSettings invariant regression, un-pin)

FR-6.title: Library grid mobile density
FR-6.review_weight: LIGHT single-QA gate -- scoped CSS-only change; aspect-ratio/object-fit already shipped, only the mobile minmax() floor needs further tightening. On-device Dean pass is the arbiter for "comfortable" density.
files: public/css/style.css (mobile .video-grid rule only -- desktop rule outside the media query untouched)

FR-7.title: Extra-deliberate delete for local (non-yt-dlp) files
FR-7.review_weight: HEAVY two-reviewer gate (destructive-action class) -- adversarial review must confirm the yt-dlp-vs-local detection signal can never fail toward LESS friction than today.
files: public/js/common.js (shared detection predicate, if placed here) or a per-page equivalent
public/js/watch.js (escalated confirm on the watch-page delete button)
public/js/main.js (escalated confirm on the home/library card two-tap delete)

FR-8.title: Download retry button + clearer active-download status (corner chip)
FR-8.review_weight: LIGHT-TO-MEDIUM gate -- reuses the existing GET /api/subscriptions/status polling data (lib/ytdlp/activity.js); no new backend polling primitive. A small server addition may be needed for one-shot retry re-invocation.
files: lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js (contextual Retry on a failed subscription row; one-shot job Retry, which does not exist today -- only Dismiss does)
lib/ytdlp/index.js (possible small addition for one-shot retry re-invocation)
public/js/common.js (new app-wide status chip component, so it's visible outside /subscriptions)
public/css/style.css (chip styling, era tokens, coordinated with the FR-2 audio dock so they never overlap on mobile)

FR-9.title: Theatre mode (video)
FR-9.review_weight: LIGHT gate. Mostly desktop; on-device Dean pass is the arbiter, especially for mobile behavior.
files: public/js/watch.js (theatre toggle -- watch-VIEW-only, never applies to the v1.16 DOCKED state)

FR-10.title: README refresh
FR-10.review_weight: PROCESS-only -- docs-only change, no functional/code risk. Fully independent of every other FR (zero code-file overlap); can run any time in parallel.

pe_design_items: Route to Design after Discovery. FR-2/FR-3/FR-4/FR-5/FR-7/FR-8/FR-9 need PE design; FR-1/FR-6/FR-10 do not (trivial/scoped fixes) but ride through Design for one cohesive plan. Key open engineering questions for PE, in order of weight: (1) FR-2's exact custom control-bar component structure (shared by audio+video across all four HTML shells' #player-host-template), the seek visual-scrub-vs-commit mechanism, the volume localStorage clamp/apply-before-playback sequencing, and the iOS-volume-constraint UI treatment (hide vs. inert); (2) FR-1's exact poll-clobber fix mechanism (skip-the-open-row vs. targeted partial update vs. another approach) -- must land independently of FR-3's timing; (3) FR-3's settings-detail surface (dedicated page vs. bottom-sheet) and how it structurally avoids FR-1's race by construction; (4) FR-5's exact new pin-store shape/location and its routes (gated, disabled-module no-op preserved), plus the resolved channelDir+label-snapshot keying; (5) FR-7's exact detection-predicate placement (shared helper vs. duplicated per-page) and the escalated-confirm UX (distinct from the existing modal/two-tap arm); (6) FR-8's exact one-shot-retry re-invocation mechanism (may need a small lib/ytdlp/index.js addition) and the app-wide status-chip component's mount point (likely common.js, since it must be visible outside /subscriptions) plus its coordination with the FR-2 audio dock; (7) FR-9's theatre-mode CSS/layout approach and its interaction with the v1.16 persistent-shell watch view and the related-items sidebar. See the exec plan's 'Recommended task grouping' section for file-overlap-driven sequencing hints (not a finalized wave plan -- EM's job in Tasks stage).
cross_cutting: Disabled-yt-dlp-module no-op preserved end-to-end for FR-1/FR-3/FR-4/FR-5/FR-8 (no new route/UI/DOM reachable with FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED off). Era-theme CSS vars for all new styling (FR-2/FR-3/FR-6/FR-8/FR-9). No new npm dependencies; CommonJS server; vanilla DOM client code; node:test covers every new pure helper; lint 0 warnings. textContent (never innerHTML) for every new dynamic string. Node 22 toolchain throughout. No headless-browser/E2E infrastructure exists (docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- Dean's on-device pass is the documented arbiter for FR-2/FR-6/FR-9/FR-3 feel, not a testing gap. Review tiers: HEAVIEST for FR-2; HEAVY for FR-3/FR-5/FR-7; LIGHT for FR-1/FR-4/FR-6/FR-9/FR-10; LIGHT-TO-MEDIUM for FR-8.

coordinator_decisions.editable_name: RESOLVED READ-ONLY this round. validateSubscriptionPatch/updateSubscription do NOT accept a name patch, and editing name would change resolveChannelDir and orphan downloads. FR-3's settings sheet edits count/quality/type/skip-shorts/filetype ONLY; the channel name renders read-only in the sheet header. AC21's 'name' field is intentionally deferred (would need a scoped server change, Out of scope).
coordinator_decisions.fr1_folded_into_fr3: RESOLVED. The standalone FR-1 count-edit hotfix is FOLDED INTO the FR-3 rearchitect (T3). T3 rebuilds the subscriptions client with a settings SHEET + a targeted in-place status-poll update (updates only each row's .sub-row-status text rather than clear+rebuild), which STRUCTURALLY eliminates the poll-clobber race by construction. There is NO separate T1 hotfix task; FR-1's ACs (AC1-AC5) are satisfied within T3. .state/inbox/software-developer-T1.md is a stale v1.20 leftover and is superseded (do not run).

wave_plan.serialization_rule: HARD RULE (coordinator-enforced): NO two CONCURRENTLY-running tasks may edit the same file -- NOT EVEN different regions/sections of the same file. Region/section ownership is NOT permitted on this shared working tree (it risks lost-update collisions between concurrent agents). Consequence: public/css/style.css is touched by 7 of 8 tasks (T2,T3,T4,T5,T6,T7,T8), so those 7 are FULLY SERIALIZED one-at-a-time. Only T9 (README-only) is style.css-free and may run in parallel -- paired with the longest pole T2 in Wave 1. All other shared files (subscriptions.js, common.js, index.js, watch.js, the two HTML shells) are likewise strictly serialized across waves.
wave_plan.note: style.css forces near-total serialization. T9 (README) is the only non-CSS task, paired with T2 (longest pole) in Wave 1. T4 (one CSS rule) gets its own wave (it can only overlap T9, already consumed by W1); it MAY optionally be folded into an adjacent task's SAME session (one agent, sequential edits, no concurrency) to save a wave, at the cost of mixing a LIGHT task into a heavier session -- kept separate here for clean review. Order respects every dep: T3(W2) before T5(W4)/T7(W6); T5(W4) before T7(W6); T6(W5) before T7(W6) and T8(W7).

per_hot_file_serialization_proof.public/css/style.css: T2,T3,T4,T5,T6,T7,T8 -- STRICT one-editor-at-a-time: T2(W1) -> T3(W2) -> T4(W3) -> T5(W4) -> T6(W5) -> T7(W6) -> T8(W7). Never concurrent.
per_hot_file_serialization_proof.lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html: T2,T3 -- STRICT: T2 player-host-template (W1) -> T3 #view-root IA (W2). W1<W2, never concurrent.
per_hot_file_serialization_proof.public/watch.html: T2,T8 -- STRICT: T2 player-host-template (W1) -> T8 theatre button (W7). Never concurrent.
per_hot_file_serialization_proof.lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js: T3,T5,T7 -- STRICT: T3 full rewrite (W2) -> T5 star toggle (W4) -> T7 retry (W6). Never concurrent.
per_hot_file_serialization_proof.public/js/common.js: T5,T6,T7 -- STRICT: T5 pins render (W4) -> T6 predicate+hardmodal (W5) -> T7 chip (W6). Never concurrent.
per_hot_file_serialization_proof.lib/ytdlp/index.js: T5,T7 -- STRICT: T5 pin routes (W4) -> T7 one-shot retry params (W6). Never concurrent.
per_hot_file_serialization_proof.public/js/watch.js: T6,T8 -- STRICT + dep: T6 local-delete branch (W5) -> T8 theatre toggle (W7). Never concurrent.
per_hot_file_serialization_proof.wave_disjointness: W1: T2 (player.js, index/setup/watch/subscriptions.html, style.css) and T9 (README.md) share NO file. W2-W7 each run exactly one task -> trivially disjoint. No two concurrent tasks share ANY file.

tasks.title: Audio/video player overhaul — custom blocky theme-aware controls
tasks.description: Remove native controls from the shared <video id=media-player>; add one shared blocky control bar (play/pause, seek, time, mute, volume, fullscreen) + click-cover-art play/pause glyph, wired once in wireHostListeners so it travels with the persistent host across FULL/DOCKED/CLOSED. Era-token/dark-mode CSS; seek input(scrub)/change(commit) split with rAF fill; persist volume with iOS volumeIsSettable feature-detect degrade; desktop fullscreen retargets to .player-container. PRESERVE every existing player feature for BOTH audio and video.
tasks.gate: HEAVIEST two-reviewer/adversarial + Dean iOS on-device arbiter
tasks.done_when: Native controls removed; custom bar drives audio+video with all v1.16 behaviors preserved; clampVolume + seekCommitTarget unit-tested; iOS volume degrades silently; lint 0 + Node22 tests pass.

tasks.title: Subscriptions-FIRST rearchitect + subscribed-date/clickable link + count-edit fix
tasks.description: List-first IA (subscriptions list primary; add/one-off demoted to <details> below); one dense row = avatar/letter + name + one muted meta line + trailing kebab. Row tap -> /?root=<channelDir>. Kebab -> in-shell bottom-sheet settings editing count/quality/type/skip-shorts/filetype (name read-only) that PATCHes the existing endpoint. Targeted in-place status-poll render (only .sub-row-status text) structurally eliminates FR-1's poll-clobber race and makes the count edit persist. formatSubscribedDate(addedAt) and a real <a href> channel link (FR-4).
tasks.done_when: List-first IA shipped; kebab bottom-sheet PATCHes and count edit survives >2.5s poll; row tap opens channel; subscribed-date + clickable link render via textContent/.href; disabled-module still 404s; lint 0 + Node22 tests pass.

tasks.title: Library grid mobile density (2 columns on phones)
tasks.description: Add a narrow phone breakpoint (@media max-width:480px) forcing .video-grid to repeat(2,1fr) for a deterministic 2 comfortable columns. Desktop rule (minmax(210px,1fr), outside the media query) untouched. CSS-only.
tasks.done_when: Mobile grid shows 2 columns at <=480px; desktop rule unchanged; no card jitter; lint 0.

tasks.title: Pin a channel playlist to persistent nav (new gated pin store)
tasks.description: New gated db.ytdlp.pins array of {id,channelDir,label,pinnedAt} snapshots (id=getMediaId(channelDir)); ensureYtdlp backfills pins:[]. 3 gated routes (GET/POST/DELETE /api/subscriptions/pins) with channelDir confined under downloadDir + bounded label. Star/pin toggle on the FR-3 row; pinned channels render ALONGSIDE (never merged into) the db.folders-driven Playlists sheet/sidebar. NEVER touch db.folders/folderSettings; POST /api/config must never read/write/prune the pin store.
tasks.done_when: Pins persist server-side across restart; star toggle works on the FR-3 row; regression test proves POST /api/config never touches db.ytdlp.pins and pins never enter db.folders; disabled-module 404s; lint 0 + Node22 tests pass.

tasks.title: Extra-deliberate delete for local (non-yt-dlp) files
tasks.description: Fail-safe pure isYtdlpManagedItem(item) predicate (true ONLY on a non-empty channelUrl/channelId/channelName; any absence/ambiguity -> local/irreplaceable) + shared checkbox-gated showHardDeleteModal (textContent, red hard-warning, Delete disabled until 'cannot be recovered' checkbox ticked). Wire into watch delete and the card second-tap for LOCAL files only; yt-dlp files keep existing showConfirmModal/two-tap unchanged. DELETE /api/videos/:id unchanged (client confirm-flow escalation only).
tasks.gate: HEAVY two-reviewer (destructive; adversarial must confirm predicate can never reduce friction)
tasks.done_when: Predicate fail-safe unit-tested (pre-v1.20 file -> local); hard-delete modal reachable on watch + card for local files; yt-dlp flows byte-unchanged; DELETE endpoint untouched; lint 0 + Node22 tests pass.

tasks.title: Download retry + app-wide status chip
tasks.description: One-shot retry re-POSTs /api/ytdlp/download from three additive fields (format/quality/filetype) added to the ephemeral one-shot activity entry; subscription retry reuses /repull contextually on a failed row. New app-wide bottom-LEFT status chip (common.js) reusing GET /api/subscriptions/status (no new backend primitive): collapsed 'N downloading . X%', expands to per-item panel; completed auto-dismiss, errors sticky; suppressed on /subscriptions; positioned to clear the FR-2 dock + bottom-nav on mobile.
tasks.done_when: One-shot Retry re-invokes via re-POST; contextual sub Retry present; chip visible app-wide while downloading, coordinates with dock, auto-dismiss/sticky correct; textContent only; lint 0 + Node22 tests pass.

tasks.title: Theatre mode (video, watch-view + desktop only)
tasks.description: Theatre toggle button in the watch view adds a theater-mode class to .watch-container widening .watch-main and stacking .watch-sidebar at desktop widths only. WATCH-VIEW layout class only — the DOCKED mini-player is never affected; the existing @media max-width:1024px sidebar-stacking is untouched; toggle hidden on phones. Optional localStorage['ft-theater'] persistence applied on watch init().
tasks.done_when: Toggle widens/restores layout at desktop widths; docked state unaffected; sidebar reconciles; hidden/safe on mobile; optional persistence works; lint 0.

tasks.title: README refresh
tasks.description: Rewrite the stale v1.10-era Features list to cover everything shipped since v1.10 (yt-dlp subscriptions + subscribe-from-downloads + per-channel playlists, docked mini-player/SPA-lite nav, prev/next + autoplay-next, download-to-device, iOS transcoding, PWA icons, quicker delete) plus this release's headline items. Screenshots are Dean's to provide (flagged, not blocking). Docs-only.
tasks.done_when: Features list and stale copy reflect the actual shipped feature set through v1.21; screenshot placeholders flagged as Dean's; no code change.

history.note: TASKS finalized by ENGINEERING-MANAGER. Read the PE ## Design + proposed task/wave breakdown, docs/ui-research-2026-07.md, and .state/feature-state.json. Confirmed by grep that #player-host-template is replicated in all FOUR shells (public/index.html, public/setup.html, public/watch.html, lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html) -- so T2 genuinely shares subscriptions.html with T3 and watch.html with T8. Recorded coordinator decisions: (1) editable subscription name = READ-ONLY this round; (2) FR-1 count-edit fix FOLDED INTO FR-3/T3 (no standalone T1). Finalized 8 tasks T2-T9 (FR-1 folded). Definitive wave plan honoring the hard 'no two concurrent tasks share ANY file' rule via STRICT one-editor-at-a-time serialization for EVERY shared file (region/section ownership NOT permitted per coordinator correction 2026-07-08 -- concurrent edits to different sections of the same file still risk lost-update collisions on this shared tree). Because public/css/style.css is touched by 7 of 8 tasks (T2,T3,T4,T5,T6,T7,T8), those 7 fully serialize; only T9 (README) is CSS-free and parallelizable. Waves: W1 = T2 + T9 (disjoint: player.js/4 shells/style.css vs README.md); W2 = T3; W3 = T4; W4 = T5; W5 = T6; W6 = T7; W7 = T8. Deps honored: T3(W2)->T5(W4)->T7(W6); T6(W5)->T7(W6)/T8(W7). Wrote per-hot-file serialization proof into wave_plan. Overwrote stale v1.20 inbox files with self-contained software-developer-T2..T9.md (Node 22 toolchain path noted; coordinator owns all git; SDEs report files-changed + lint/test only, do NOT commit); marked software-developer-T1.md superseded. Set stage=implementation, current_wave=1, current_tasks=[T2,T9]. Filled the exec plan's ## Task breakdown section. Wrote no application code, touched no git. Wave 1 (T2, T9) ready to launch.

PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Acceptance for v1.21.0 'The Polish Release'. #audio-bg-art, seekCommitTarget's live-mode fallback removed), lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js in full (FR-1/FR-3/FR-4/FR-5-client -- createSubscriptionRow, buildSettingsSheet, PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Discovery for v1.21.0 'The Polish Release' (feature/v1.21-polish-release, off main at v1.20.0) -- a large, 10-FR release bundling all of Dean's post-v1.20 feedback. Read docs/CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/RELIABILITY.md, and docs/ui-research-2026-07.md (the mandated UI/UX research synthesis covering subscriptions/library grid/playlists-pinning/audio-player/download-status) FIRST as instructed, then ROADMAP.md's Planned section (confirmed every FR below already appears there -- no scope creep), docs/exec-plans/tech-debt-tracker.md (no open item addressed/created), and the completed 2026-07-06-v1.16-watch-experience.md + 2026-07-08-v1.20-subscribe.md exec plans for precedent/style. Confirmed no active exec plan currently exists to conflict with. Grounded every FR directly in the live code (not assumed): public/js/player.js in full (the v1.16 persistent player controller -- central to FR-2 and FR-9), public/watch.html + lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html shell markup, lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js in full (central to FR-1/FR-3/FR-4/FR-5/FR-8), lib/ytdlp/store.js's validateSubscriptionPatch/updateSubscription/addSubscription, lib/ytdlp/index.js's PATCH route, lib/ytdlp/activity.js in full, server.js's POST /api/config folder/folderSettings pruning logic + DELETE /api/videos/:id, watch.js's delete-button handler, main.js's card-delete two-tap-arm code, and style.css's grid/thumbnail/audio-mode sections. Key grounded findings (full detail in the exec plan's Decision log): (1) FR-1's actual root cause is a client-side render-timing race -- the subscriptions list's ~2.5s live-status poll unconditionally rebuilds every row, destroying an open/unsaved edit panel; the server-side validator/mutator path was read in full and is already correct; (2) FR-5 cannot reuse folderSettings as a pin store -- POST /api/config prunes any folderSettings key that isn't a real db.folders entry or the one synthetic Downloads root, so a per-channel pin needs a NEW, separate persisted store; (3) FR-6's aspect-ratio/object-fit ask is already shipped, and a v1.15.0 mobile grid-tightening rule already exists -- scope narrowed to further tightening per Dean's continued on-device report; (4) FR-2 has a genuine, non-fixable iOS Safari platform constraint (script cannot set HTMLMediaElement.volume) that must be documented, not chased as a bug; (5) FR-7 can reuse the existing v1.20 FR-2 channelUrl/channelId/channelName signal for yt-dlp-vs-local detection, with an explicit fail-safe requirement; (6) FR-4's addedAt field already exists since v1.11.0 -- no new stored field needed; (7) FR-8's one-shot download jobs have genuinely no retry today (only Dismiss), the larger of the two retry gaps. Two forks resolved non-blocking (FR-2's shared audio+video control-bar scope; FR-5's channelDir+label-snapshot pin keying); no genuinely blocking fork surfaced.
